summary: ESPN athlete resolution must not filter common/v3/search by sport=<name> (that returns count:0); use type=player, or the richer multi-sport search/v2 endpoint. Per-athlete stats/splits/recent-form come from .../athletes/<id>/overview.
reason: The generated ESPN lookup queried common/v3/search with sport=soccer, which the endpoint answers with count:0 (it wants type=player), producing a 100% 'no ESPN athlete result' miss. We moved to search/v2 (live, multi-sport, typed rows) and guard resolution on soccer+name-match to avoid resolving the wrong athlete since search/v2 has no sport filter. A doctor canary flags any future silent drift.
validated_outcome: player 'joao neves' returns sources.espn ok with athleteId 355061, non-empty season stats and per-competition splits (Portugal teamId 482), and recent games; team reports enrich ESPN for the top players by market value within a budget; doctor's ESPN Source canary reports healthy.
